ops.title: Workflow tool `args` can arrive JSON-encoded as a string — agent(args.prompt) then dispatches an empty prompt; inline long prompts in the script instead
ops.body:
Passing a long prompt to a Workflow via the `args` parameter (`args: {"prompt": "..."}`) failed silently: args reached the script as a JSON-encoded STRING, `args.prompt` was undefined, and the agent ran with an empty prompt — it returned "no task provided" in 8s and the workflow "completed" successfully.

**Why:** the harness may re-encode the args value; the workflow completes green, so the only symptom is a nonsense agent result and a suspiciously short duration.

**How to apply:** embed prompts in the workflow script itself as a `const PROMPT = \`...\`` template literal (escape internal backticks); reserve `args` for small scalars, and if a workflow returns instantly with an off-task result, check the journal for what the agent actually received before re-diagnosing. Related: [[workflow-structured-output-keep-small]].
